content: Lipid-lowering therapy algorithm: First-line high-intensity statin for all with clinical ASCVD or high risk. If LDL remains above threshold on maximally tolerated statin, add ezetimibe (inhibits intestinal cholesterol absorption—IMPROVE-IT showed additional CV benefit). If still above threshold, add PCSK9 inhibitor (alirocumab, evolocumab—monoclonal antibodies that increase LDL receptor recycling; FOURIER and ODYSSEY OUTCOMES showed ~15% relative risk reduction per 39 mg/dL LDL reduction). Inclisiran (siRNA targeting PCSK9) allows twice-yearly dosing after initial doses. Bempedoic acid (ATP citrate lyase inhibitor) oral alternative; CLEAR OUTCOMES showed CV benefit but increased gout/amputation risk. Bile acid sequestrants rarely used (GI side effects). Fibrates for severe hypertriglyceridemia (>500 mg/dL to prevent pancreatitis); no consistent CV benefit when added to statins (ACCORD-LIPID). Icosapent ethyl (pure EPA) reduced CV events in REDUCE-IT trial (high-risk patients with TG 135-499 on statins).
